So Ticketmaster stock crashes the SECOND Taylor Swift says she's done touring, and we're supposed to believe this is just normal market reaction? The algorithm vampires who've been feeding off Swifties' wallets for years knew this was coming.

Live Nation suddenly has 2.8 billion dollars worth of venues they don't need anymore, which is convenient timing for their mysterious offshore shell companies to swoop in and buy everything at bargain prices. Nothing suspicious about corporate musical chairs here.

All these merchandise suppliers getting laid off at once makes you wonder who's really pulling the strings in the concert industrial complex. Follow the money from those limited edition hoodies straight to the same investment firms that own everything else.

Hotels canceling 40% of their bookings because ONE artist retires from touring tells you everything about how artificially propped up this whole economy is. The house of cards was held together by friendship bracelets and glitter.

Airlines are literally changing flight schedules because Taylor Swift won't be performing, proving that our entire transportation infrastructure revolves around manufactured pop culture events. Wake up, people.

Even Uber's precious algorithms are having an existential crisis without concert surges, which means they've been harvesting our movement data to predict entertainment industry trends this whole time. Your ride to Starbucks is corporate espionage.

A BURRITO TRUCK raised their prices because they lost some concert customers, and I am ABSOLUTELY LOSING MY MIND over this because it's just a BURRITO, it costs thirty cents in ingredients, but somehow Taylor Swift's retirement means my Tuesday lunch is now a mortgage payment!
